macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Equities Hit Record Highs as Geopolitical Tensions Ease
items.summary: The S&P 500 surpassed 7,230 and the Dow approached 50,000 as diplomatic breakthroughs with Iran reduced geopolitical risk premiums. The rally was powered by strong earnings momentum from mega-cap tech stocks and relief from lower oil prices.

items.title: Oil Prices Retreat Following US-Iran De-escalation
items.summary: Crude oil prices dipped following diplomatic breakthroughs and statements to pause Hormuz operations, though prices remain structurally supported above the $100 level. The decline provided relief to markets and broadened equity rallies beyond tech into cyclical sectors.

items.title: US Corporate Earnings Revised Higher for 2026
items.summary: US corporate earnings are being revised upward into 2026, with the 'Magnificent Seven' tech stocks accounting for a significant portion of expected earnings growth. This earnings momentum is driving the current market rally.

items.title: Q1 GDP Growth Moderates to 2.0% Annualized Rate
items.summary: First-quarter real GDP grew at a 2.0% annualized rate following a 0.5% gain in Q4, with over 90% of gains coming from business fixed investment in equipment and intellectual property. Capex growth is expected to continue but decelerate through 2026-2027.

items.title: Tariff Protectionism Appears to Have Peaked
items.summary: The administration's proposed 15% tariff rate was never implemented, and even the 10% rate is facing multiple court challenges. Analysts believe the high-water mark for protectionism has passed.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Breaks Above $80,000, Altcoin Rally Expected
items.summary: Bitcoin has officially broken above the $80,000 level, marking a significant technical breakout that could trigger a broader altcoin rally. Analysts identify $86,000 as the next key resistance level, with several altcoins including Cardano, Avalanche, and Solana showing strength as momentum builds.

items.title: Clarity Act Cryptocurrency Legislation Expected to Move Forward
items.summary: Senator Bernie Moreno announced plans to mark up the Bitcoin and crypto market structure Clarity Act next week, with Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse indicating high likelihood of passage if the bill clears committee this month. The legislation is expected to advance either next week or the week after.

items.title: Coinbase CEO Announces Job Cuts Amid Industry Consolidation
items.summary: Coinbase CEO announced significant job cuts as the cryptocurrency industry continues to consolidate. The announcement comes as major figures like Jack Mallers back merger plans and discuss Bitcoin infrastructure development.

items.title: MicroStrategy Reports $12.54 Billion Q1 Loss
items.summary: MicroStrategy, a major Bitcoin holder, reported a substantial $12.54 billion loss in Q1, raising concerns about potential Bitcoin sales to fund dividend obligations and signaling potential market pressure.

items.title: Bitcoin ETF Inflows Support Price as Short-Term Holders Take Profits
items.summary: Real spot demand and ETF inflows are supporting Bitcoin's price near $80,000, though nearly 30,000 BTC in profits were sent to exchanges by short-term holders over the last 24 hours, indicating some profit-taking activity.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Updates ChatGPT with GPT-5.5 Instant as Default Model
items.summary: OpenAI announced on May 5, 2026 that GPT-5.5 Instant is now ChatGPT's default model, with internal evaluations showing 52.5% fewer hallucinated claims on high-stakes prompts and 37.3% fewer inaccurate claims on flagged conversations. The update includes new memory controls and broader use of past chats, uploaded files, and Gmail for personalization.

items.title: US Government Gains Pre-Release Access to Tech Giants' AI Models
items.summary: The US government announced on May 5, 2026 that it will have access to evaluate new AI models from Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and xAI before their public release through partnerships with the Commerce Department's Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI). This policy shift follows the emergence of Anthropic's powerful Mythos model and represents a renegotiation of agreements originally made under the Biden administration.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Modern Warfare 4 Playtesting Confirmed on PS4
items.summary: Data miners have uncovered evidence that Call of Duty's next installment, rumored to be Modern Warfare 4, is currently undergoing playtesting on PS4 despite earlier reports suggesting the game would drop previous-generation console support.

items.title: Clash of Clans Limited-Time Golden Dragon Event
items.summary: Clash of Clans introduced a 24-hour event featuring 20 Golden Dragons in every army, creating an overpowered troop composition that temporarily broke game balance with the boss unit dealing 1,400 DPS.

items.title: Rise of Kingdoms Kingdom 1093 Converts to Single-Alliance System
items.summary: Rise of Kingdoms' kingdom 1093 announced a major shift from multi-alliance to single-alliance structure, marking what players consider the end of an era in the game's endgame strategy.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Anthropic Plans $200B Spend on Google Cloud and Chips Over Five Years
items.summary: Anthropic announced plans to spend approximately $200 billion on Google's cloud infrastructure and chips over the next five years, representing over 40% of Google's disclosed revenue backlog.

items.title: AMD Reports Q1 Revenue Up 38% YoY to $10.25B, Data Center Revenue Surges 57%
items.summary: Advanced Micro Devices exceeded expectations with Q1 revenue of $10.25B and data center revenue reaching $5.8B, with Q2 forecasts also beating estimates, driving a 7%+ after-hours stock jump.

items.title: Super Micro Computer Reports Q3 Revenue Up 123% YoY to $10.24B
items.summary: Super Micro Computer forecasted Q4 revenue and adjusted profit above Wall Street estimates, capitalizing on robust demand and driving a 17%+ after-hours stock surge.

items.title: Micron Surges 11% as Highest-Capacity SSD Begins Shipping, Market Cap Exceeds $700B
items.summary: Micron Technology's stock jumped 11% following the announcement that its highest-capacity SSD has started shipping, pushing the company's market capitalization past $700 billion for the first time.

items.title: Nvidia Invests $2 Billion in AI Cloud Firm Nebius
items.summary: Nvidia announced a $2 billion investment in Nebius, an AI cloud computing firm, expanding its presence in the cloud infrastructure market.

items.title: Germany-Based eleQtron Raises €57M Series A for Quantum Computing
items.summary: eleQtron, a German company developing trapped-ion quantum computing processors, secured €57 million in Series A funding led by Schwarz Digits.
